Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify connection path in architecture diagram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

commit.message:
fix(auth): server-side email verification via /auth/confirm

Supabase email links used the token hash flow but /auth/callback
expected PKCE code flow, causing all email links to redirect to
homepage instead of the intended destination.

- New /auth/confirm route: verifies token_hash server-side via
  verifyOtp(), creates session, redirects correctly
  (recovery -> /reset-password, confirmation -> /dashboard)
- Updated Supabase email templates (confirmation, recovery,
  magic link) to use /auth/confirm?token_hash={{.TokenHash}}
- Script execution/update-email-templates.ts for future updates

fix: deduplicate album replies using media_group_id

When a channel posts an album (multiple photos/videos), Telegram
forwards each item as a separate message with is_automatic_forward,
causing the bot to reply multiple times. Track seen media_group_id
per group and skip duplicates so only the first message gets a reply.

Harden firewall from red team analysis

DENY additions:
- Privilege escalation variants (doas, pkexec, su, run0)
- Shell execution of file paths (sh /tmp/x.sh, bash ./script.sh)
- Self-tampering guard (Tier 1e): blocks writes to ~/.claude/hooks/,
  settings.json, and rules/ via cp, mv, sed -i, tee, ln, redirects

ALLOW tightening:
- Remove gh api (arbitrary GitHub API calls, now falls to ASK)
- Remove open/xdg-open (arbitrary URL opening, now falls to ASK)

Bug fix:
- Newline-in-segment bypass: grep -cq was always returning true,
  breaking all ALLOW pattern matching. Fixed to use [[ == *newline* ]]

Documentation:
- Add threat model section to README (friendly-agent assumption)
- Add structured red team findings table (redteam.md)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(browser): use Xvfb in stealth mode + CDP UA override (#71)

* fix(browser): use Xvfb display in stealth mode to avoid HeadlessChrome fingerprint

When stealth mode is enabled and a DISPLAY is available (Xvfb), Chrome now
launches non-headless to avoid the HeadlessChrome user agent string that
aggressive anti-bot systems detect. Also adds CDP-based user agent override
and navigator.webdriver removal after launch, plus --proxy flag support in
the CLI.

* Bump version to 0.1.60

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump version to 0.1.61

commit.message:
feat(football): expand Ron Atkinson openers and Micah Richards BRILLIANT pool

Ron: adds new opener patterns from user examples — 'Well for me Clive',
chuckling dismissals, mild outrage ('You've got to be kidding me'),
old-fashioned exclamations (Good grief / Heavens). Adds NON-SEQUITUR
OPENER pool — Ron arrives from La Manga, a prawn, a kip, a call with
Fergie, Dean Saunders unprompted, Cyrille at The Hawthorns. All delivered
without apology. The pivot back to football is thin or nonexistent.

Micah: BRILLIANT rotation pool added — single words (PHENOMENAL,
SENSATIONAL, STUPENDOUS etc), phrase pool (SICK, FIRE, GOATED,
ABSOLUTE SCENES), dead phrase pool (COWABUNGA, RADICAL, BODACIOUS,
TUBULAR, GNARLY) — all delivered with total sincerity. If challenged
on COWABUNGA: genuine confusion. He likes it. It means something to him.

commit.message:
Fix HUD drawdown double-counting W/D when multiple buckets per account

The previous approach computed Adjusted Value per row (Total Value - Cum_All_WD),
then summed across rows for the same account+date. Since Cum_All_WD is the same
for all rows of an account, the W/D adjustment was multiplied by the bucket count.

Fix: aggregate Total Value and W/D separately per date, then apply the cumulative
W/D offset after grouping, so each withdrawal is counted exactly once.

commit.message:
fix: block geolocation for bots to prevent Mountain View, CA URLs

Google's crawler IP resolves to Mountain View, CA via the geolocation
API, generating ~2,070 invalid URLs with estado=CA that get marked as
noindex, wasting crawl budget.

- Add isBot() utility to detect crawlers by user-agent
- Skip IP geolocation in GeoLocationContext for bots
- Skip router.replace in SearchResultClient for bots

commit.message:
Sync lab pipeline and HRT blood draw schedule bidirectionally

The lab pipeline and HRT blood draw schedule were two independent
systems with no data exchange. Now they stay in sync automatically:

- matchDrawsToLogs checks lab protocol statuses (Priority 3)
- Logging a blood draw on HRT schedule advances the lab to
  blood_draw_complete
- Advancing a lab in the pipeline logs the blood draw for the
  HRT protocol
- Undo works in both directions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent duplicate SMS reminders by writing dedup log in mutation

Move the sitterSmsLog write from the async action into the
checkAndSendReminders mutation, before scheduling the action. Since
Convex mutations are transactional, the next cron tick always sees the
log entry and skips — closing the race window where multiple actions
could be scheduled before any wrote the log.

commit.message:
chore: run PR checks on PRs targeting dev (#38)

* fix: ttyd auth default-on, gh credential helper, Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK (#33)

* fix: ttyd auth default-on, gh credential helper, Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK

- (#4) Flip ttyd auth logic: credentials now required by default; container
  exits with a clear error if neither creds nor TTYD_NO_AUTH=true are set.
  Added TTYD_NO_AUTH=true as the explicit opt-out for unauthenticated access.
- (#14) Add `gh auth setup-git` to entrypoint so git push/fetch works
  seamlessly via GH_TOKEN without embedding tokens in remote URLs.
- (#10) Add HEALTHCHECK to Dockerfile — curls ttyd every 30s with a 10s
  startup grace period and 3 retries before marking the container unhealthy.
- Updated .env.example to document new ttyd auth defaults and TTYD_NO_AUTH.

Closes #4, #10, #14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #33

- gh auth setup-git is now best-effort: skips gracefully if GH_TOKEN is
  absent or gh is unauthenticated, so the web terminal can still start
  without GitHub credentials.
- Fix TTYD auth precedence: credentials now take priority over
  TTYD_NO_AUTH=true; setting both is treated as a hard config error
  rather than silently disabling auth.
- HEALTHCHECK now respects TTYD_BASE_PATH so it doesn't produce false
  unhealthy status when a non-root base path is configured.
- HEALTHCHECK validates TTYD_PORT is numeric before use to prevent
  shell injection via environment variable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: itscooleric <itscooleric@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gosu privilege drop for firewall + compose hardening (#34)

* fix: gosu privilege drop for firewall, compose hardening

Fixes the silent firewall no-op (#30) caused by iptables requiring
geteuid()==0 even with CAP_NET_ADMIN when the container runs as the
non-root clide user.

- (#30) Install gosu; set Dockerfile back to USER root so entrypoints
  start as root. All entrypoints (firewall.sh, claude-entrypoint.sh,
  entrypoint.sh) now apply any root-only setup first (iptables, config
  writes) then exec gosu clide to drop privileges before the workload.
  The firewall is now always effective when NET_ADMIN is present.
- (#30) Hardcode HOME_DIR=/home/clide in claude-entrypoint.sh to avoid
  resolving to /root when running as root; chown .claude.json to clide
  after writing.
- (#30) gosu clide gh auth setup-git in entrypoint.sh so gh config
  writes to clide's home, not root's.
- (#6) Add cap_drop: ALL, security_opt: no-new-privileges:true, and
  resource guardrails (mem_limit: 4g, cpus: 4.0, pids_limit: 512) to
  the docker-compose.yml base anchor. cap_add: NET_ADMIN is preserved
  for the firewall.

Closes #30, #6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: suppress DL3002 for intentional USER root before gosu drop

The last USER is root by design — entrypoints apply iptables rules as
root then exec gosu clide to drop privileges before the workload starts.
Suppress hadolint DL3002 with a pragma rather than papering over the
intentional pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: itscooleric <itscooleric@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
